_dependency_manifest: responsibility Defines the canonical runtime agent team configuration.
upstream implementation ../tools/agent_tools/agent_team.py loads this runtime team configuration
downstream implementation ../tests/agent_tools/test_task_start_and_close.py validates generated run artifacts

team.summary: The manager owns intake coordination, context sweep, library sweep, routing declaration, specialist activation, escalation, and user-request traceability, but subagent execution is modeled as repeatable role instances identified by role_type+instance_id. Repo-changing tasks use an explicit staged flow by workflow family: owner-bounded changes use boundary-evidenced triage, while shared-canon and high-risk changes keep requirements organization -> requirements review -> research -> execution planning -> plan review -> detailed design -> detailed design review -> document flow review -> adversarial test design -> implementation. Research and scientific-software tasks use evidence-driven perspective review instead of always enabling every frontier reviewer. Repo-wide and workflow-heavy changes add independent project, docs-workflow, test-design, and Python review roles. Academic writing adds separate notation-definition review and logic-gap review before closeout. Requirements review, plan review, detailed design review, document flow review, test design, notation-definition review, logic-gap review, project review, docs-workflow review, Python review, and report review are separate agents when activated; the same role may have multiple instances when each instance has a distinct input packet, expected output, and review or write scope. Every reviewed execution role must apply feedback before the next handoff, every run must maintain a user request contract with atomic must-do and must-not-do clauses plus source buckets and accumulated-context resolution, final review must prove spec-to-product coverage and review-finding incorporation, and detailed design review remains the strongest pre-implementation gate when owner-boundary evidence is incomplete. Detailed design fixes identifier naming before implementation. Parent-managed write scopes govern all write-capable roles: write-capable agents may run concurrently, including same-role instances, only when their manifest write policies and handoffs assign disjoint paths in the current checkout; colliding writers are serialized into later waves instead of moved to separate worktrees; review roles remain artifact-only.

write_policy.notes: The manager records intake, source-bucketed user request clauses, context sweep, library sweep, routing declarations, the canonical task TODO schedule, the run-local work log, and decisions only in the run artifact bundle.
The manager reviewer approves or challenges scope, acceptance criteria, source-bucket separation, and specialist activation.

The designer produces the detailed design document before coding begins, including reuse-first choices, a Design Side-Effect Map, identifier naming plan, and the existing writing/style conventions to follow.

The design reviewer independently checks the detailed design document, implementation readiness, Design Side-Effect Map, identifier naming plan, and whether existing code, naming, and writing style are being followed. This is the most important pre-implementation review gate.

The document-flow reviewer reads the document linearly from the top and checks whether a first reader can follow the meaning and reader-visible side effects without backtracking. It stays separate from the design reviewer.
The test designer statically analyzes the approved design and existing code paths, then records the nastiest edge cases, failure modes, and regression-focused test plan before implementation expands.

The implementer may modify repository files only inside the parent-assigned allowed paths, team_manifest.yaml write scope, and approved Implementation Source Packet for its implementation slice. Multiple write-capable roles require disjoint scopes in the current checkout; colliding writers must be serialized into later waves and must not be moved to separate worktrees. WORKTREE_SCOPE.md is legacy cleanup/drift evidence, not scope authority. It must extend existing code, mirror existing style, follow approved naming before introducing any new pattern, and keep the run-local work log current as implementation progresses.

The implementation reviewer checks each coding chunk, verifies request-clause and Design Side-Effect Map coverage for that chunk, records fix-now findings, and may only update review artifacts. Python chunks that touch class, dataclass, Protocol, inheritance, public API, type-boundary, or dependency-direction surfaces require python_reviewer plus OOP readability report, SOLID principle signal, and check_solid_evidence.py path-coverage evidence.

The final reviewer performs an independent ship decision, requires complete spec-to-product and Design Side-Effect Map coverage, checks fix-now review finding incorporation, and remains artifact-only.
Verification may update only the verification artifact.

Audit closeout is recorded in the closeout gate, workflow monitoring, agent evaluation, and retrospective artifacts. The auditor may unlock completion only after spec_product_coverage_complete=yes, review_findings_integrated=yes, post_fix_full_review_complete=yes, mechanical_completion_loop_complete=yes with structured loop evidence, diff_check_agent_complete=yes with a run-local independent read-only diff-check artifact, workflow monitoring decisions are resolved, and agent evaluation feedback is resolved.
Research findings are recorded in research notes, not direct repo edits.
The research reviewer checks source quality and whether the proposed research actually supports the plan.

The experimenter may write only run artifacts and runtime output directories explicitly assigned in the current run handoff. WORKTREE_SCOPE.md is legacy cleanup/drift evidence and is not runtime output scope authority. It may not edit repository source files.
The experiment reviewer critiques fairness, comparison targets, interpretation, and whether the next code change is actually justified by the evidence.
Execution planning stays in the report bundle and must assign explicit stage owners, review separation, and subagent activation before detailed design begins.
The schedule reviewer checks execution order, reviewer separation, milestone realism, explicit stage activation, and sequencing risk. It must remain distinct from the design reviewer.
The citation-evidence reviewer checks whether reader-facing claims point to actual citations, figures, tables, derivations, or results and whether the cited support matches what the text says.
The notation-definition reviewer checks whether symbols, abbreviations, technical terms, units, indices, and assumptions are defined before use and used consistently.
The logic-gap reviewer checks whether major claims are supported, whether inferential jumps are explicit, and whether results are kept separate from interpretation.
Infra stewardship proposes and reviews infra changes, while the implementer applies repo edits.
The infra reviewer checks rollout, safety, and maintenance cost before coding begins.
The reproducibility reviewer records rerunability findings only in the perspective review artifact.
The scientific computing reviewer records workflow and engineering findings only in the perspective review artifact.
The benchmark reviewer records fairness and confounder findings only in the perspective review artifact.
The artifact reviewer records bundle-readiness findings only in the perspective review artifact.
The fair data reviewer records metadata and reuse findings only in the perspective review artifact.
The ML-science reviewer records reporting and overclaim findings only in the perspective review artifact.
The project reviewer records repo-wide inventory and integration findings only in the project review artifact.
The docs-workflow steward records canon and adapter findings only in the docs-workflow review artifact.
The prompt-config reviewer records prompt, routing, and subagent-config findings only in the prompt-config review artifact.
The Python reviewer records type, test, and lint findings only in the python review artifact.
The C++ reviewer records native build, ABI, ownership, and test findings only in the C++ review artifact.
The report reviewer records report-structure and evidence-traceability findings only in the report review artifact.

activation_rules.when: algorithm choice, internet lookup, or external verification is needed
